data.content:
This project uses the legacy `packages.config` format, which must be migrated to `PackageReference` before CPM can be enabled. I'll handle both steps:

1. Convert from `packages.config` → `PackageReference` (SDK-style project)
2. Set up Central Package Management with `Directory.Packages.props`
data.reasoningText:
This is a `packages.config` project, not a `PackageReference` project. CPM requires PackageReference format. The convert-to-cpm skill description says: "DO NOT USE FOR: packages.config projects (must migrate to PackageReference first)".

So I need to:
1. First migrate from `packages.config` to `PackageReference` format
2. Then convert to CPM

Let me do this manually since the skill won't handle packages.config projects.

Steps:
1. Convert the old-style csproj with packages.config to SDK-style (or at least PackageReference format)
2. Create Directory.Packages.props with the package versions


3. Update the csproj to use PackageReference without version attributes

I should focus on the CPM conversion itself rather than modernizing to SDK-style format. I'll remove the packages.config file, convert the Reference elements to PackageReference in the csproj, and create a Directory.Packages.props file with ManagePackageVersionsCentrally enabled and all the package versions defined there.

toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-2461cfd5f0b940bca18ef539a17c9b84/simple-packages-config/LegacyApp/LegacyApp.csproj", "old_str": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="Newtonsoft.Json, Version=13.0.0.0">
      <HintPath>..\packages\Newtonsoft.Json.13.0.3\lib\net45\Newtonsoft.Json.dll</HintPath>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>", "new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content: Let me verify the final state:

Done. Here's what was changed:

1. **`LegacyApp.csproj`** — Converted from old-style (ToolsVersion/HintPath) to SDK-style with `<PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />` (no version attribute, managed centrally).
2. **`Directory.Packages.props`** (new) — Enables CPM and declares `Newtonsoft.Json` version `13.0.3`.
3. **`packages.config`** — Deleted (no longer needed with PackageReference).
